titleStmt.title: SII 11.180: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 180. (B.K. No. 268 of 1927-28.) KOP, BĀGALKŌṬ TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT. ON A SLAB LYING BEHIND THE TEMPLE OF DURGAVVA.

summary.p: This mutilated record belongs to the period of Vikramāditya VI and mentions Perggaḍe Muddarasa, who is described as endowed with the office of the Mahāmātya (a great minister). It mentions rājadhānī Vikramapura. We know from inscriptions that Arasibīḍi in the Hungund Taluk of the Bijapur District, was known as Vikramapura and as the secondary capital of the Western Chālukyas (See Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XV, p. 82). Since a portion of the inscription is chiselled away, it is not possible to make out the object of the record.
The inscription bears the date Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 10, Krōdhana, Āshāḍha, śu. 1, Wednesday, Dakshiṇāyana-saṅkrānti. Its English equivalent is A.D. 1085, June 25, Wednesday; ·82.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti samastarājyabharanirūWritten inB.K. No. 267 of 1927-28.
pita mahāmātyapadevīvirājamāna mānōnna ta prabhumaṁtrōtsāhaśaktitraya
saṁpannarappa śrīmatperggaḍe muddarasar cāḷukyavikramavarṣada 10 neya krōdhanasaṁva tsarada
Āṣāḍha su 1 budhavāraṁ dakṣiṇāyanasaṁkrāntiyandu rājadhāni vikramapurada śrīThe continuation is chiselled
Digital edition of SII 11.180 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
